title: McLaughlin Eastshore State Park - Wikipedia
content: # McLaughlin Eastshore State Park. | Location | San Francisco Bay Area (Alameda County, California) |. **McLaughlin Eastshore State Park** is a state park and wildlife refuge along the San Francisco Bay shoreline of the East Bay "East Bay (San Francisco Bay Area)") between the cities of Richmond, Albany, Berkeley, Emeryville, and Oakland. The specific parts of the park are Point Isabel "Point Isabel (promontory)"), Albany Beach and the bird preserve (not including Albany Bulb), the North Basin Strip, Berkeley Meadow, Brickyard Cove Staging Area, and the Emeryville Crescent. Originally named just **Eastshore State Park**, it was renamed in October 2012 to honor the late Save the Bay founder Sylvia McLaughlin, who, along with the late Dwight Steele of Citizens for Eastshore Park (now Citizens for East Shore Parks), drove the establishment of the park. Prior to 2013, it was jointly managed by the California State Parks and East Bay Regional Park District (EBRPD).

title: McLaughlin Eastshore State Park | East Bay Regional Park District
content: # McLaughlin Eastshore State Park. McLaughlin Eastshore State Park is one of the most outstanding achievements in the history of open space protection. On October 3, 2012, the California State Park and Recreation Commission renamed this 8.5-mile shoreline parkland McLaughlin Eastshore State Park in honor of Save The Bay co-founder Sylvia McLaughlin. The East Bay Regional Park District, acting as agent for the State, used funds from the Park District's 1988 Measure AA and state park bonds to acquire the property and clean up contaminated areas at a cost of more than $33 million. From I-80 (the Eastshore Freeway) and from NORTHBOUND I-580 in Richmond, take westbound Central Avenue (towards the Bay). From NORTHBOUND I-80 (the Eastshore Freeway) or from SOUTHBOUND I-580 in Albany, take Buchanan Street westbound (towards the Bay). From I-80 (the Eastshore Freeway)/I-580 in Berkeley, take westbound Gilman Street (towards the Bay).
